Headline: Cardiologist Dr. Borović: "The weakest is to drink coffee with milk."
Article_Text:
"People who enjoy coffee, enjoy caffeine, which is a crude plant base and is classified among narcotics. How harmful and unfavorable caffeine is, it is clear that it ranks fifth in the list of substances after heroin, nicotine, cocaine, and alcohol," explains Dr. Borović.

"Caffeine remains in the body from 68 to 72 hours and prevents the production of calcium and magnesium from food. The bones are continuously rebuilt, as they have cells that regenerate, others are in the process of creating new structures in bone tissue. Thus, as the bones suffer adjustments to burdens, caffeine reduces the absorption of calcium from food, which leads to lower density in bone structure. Women's bones feel the loss, especially after menopause, as they should take calcium physiologically, and since it is mostly small, they need to compensate for this deficiency. I believe, coffee also contributes to statistics, which say that one in three women and every seventh man has osteoporosis."
Narrative: The article focuses on the health implications of caffeine consumption, particularly its effects on calcium absorption and bone density, highlighting a cautionary stance towards drinking coffee with milk.

Headline: Biden has not canceled Easter for transgender people
Article_Text:
Biden has triggered a new proclamation that Easter Sunday will henceforth be celebrated as Transgender Visibility Day. However, the source of this information is unclear, as it stems from the Raskrinkavanje.ba portal, which has reportedly manipulated facts, and is the first to announce the date for the celebration of Transgender Visibility Day.

The text mentions that children of members of the U.S. National Guard are prohibited from drawing religious images in Easter celebrations at the gathering scheduled for the White House in 2024.

The text also notes that the White House has "doubled down on the reaction against religious images and warns that military families will participate in the traditional Easter egg event".  

These claims are claimed to be manipulated by some informational regions from Raskrinkavanje that also maintain the event's integrity for military personnel.
Narrative: The article conveys a critical stance towards the Biden administration's decision regarding Easter celebrations, framing it as an inappropriate direction with regards to religious freedom and traditional values.

Headline: France sends troops to Ukraine: Russian report
Article_Text:
Will the deployment of a Foreign Legion unit commanded by French officers trigger a wider European war?  
   
Editors’ Note, May 6, 2024: A link originally indicating a source for this report has gone dead. A new link to the same source, a Sputnik page, is given bellow. The author in an addendum posted on his Substack blog May 6 has discussed the sourcing. The first two paragraphs immediately below accordingly represent a rewrite to update the May 4 article, the thrust of which France has officially denied, and there are additional edits farther down in the text.  
   
(Further update, May 7, 2024: Russia’s Pravda has published a detailed article setting forth what Russian military bloggers who specialize in military matters have reported. The article, in English, is available here.)  
   
France’s decision to send Foreign Legionnaires should be seen as a peculiar French compromise. France is not deploying its home army and, besides the small number of officers, the men sent generally are not French citizens.  
   
France’s decision has two meanings, beyond the obvious one of potentially triggering a pan-European war.  
   
First of all, it indicates a lack of political will among NATO member states to face down Putin without making a real commitment.  
   
In 2022 France had a number of Ukrainians and Russians in the Foreign Legion (Légion étrangère). They were allowed to leave the Legion and, in the case of the Ukrainians, return to Ukraine to join Ukrainian forces. It isn’t clear if the Russians returned home.  
   
Today the Legion is primarily managed by French officers but the rank and file are generally foreigners. Under the current anonymat (being anonymous) rule, a volunteer who joins the Legion can decide whether to keep his given name or adopt a new one. Legionnaires serve for five-year terms, after which they can ask for French citizenship. If a legionnaire is wounded, he is entitled to gain French citizenship without any waiting period. There are no women in the Foreign Legion.  
   
France itself does not have many troops to put on Ukraine’s battle lines, should the French government want to do so. According to reports, today France cannot support an overseas deployment of a full division and won’t have this capability until 2027 at the earliest.
Narrative: The article presents a neutral analysis on France's decision to send troops to Ukraine, suggesting implications for NATO's commitment to confronting Russia without escalating tensions.

Headline: It’s ‘time to stop using terms like long COVID,’ health agency says: ‘Unnecessary fear’
Article_Text:
Follow the science — right out the door.

The term "long COVID" should be tossed aside like a stack of expired N95 masks — that’s according to health experts in one country, who found that the moniker can cause “unnecessary fear.”

Government-backed medical researchers in Australia say it’s time to stop using the fear-inducing phrase, which became popular after high volumes of people testing positive for COVID-19 led to a surge in generally non-severe “virus fatigue symptoms” that would normally have gone unnoticed, South West News Service reported.

Dr. John Gerrard, Queensland’s chief health officer, who oversaw the newly released study said, “We believe it is time to stop using terms like ‘long COVID.’”  

“They wrongly imply there is something unique and exceptional about longer-term symptoms associated with this virus,” he explained.

Sixteen percent of respondents said they were experiencing symptoms in spring 2023, while 3.6 percent reported "moderate-to-severe functional impairment" in their daily lives.

No evidence was found that adults who tested positive in 2022 were experiencing this increased level of impairment at a higher rate than those who tested negative, or those who simply had the flu.

The study noted that rates of diagnosed "long COVID" were lower than in other countries, due to tight restrictions imposed by the Australian government during the pandemic.

The finished document is due to be presented next month at 2024’s European Congress of Clinical Microbiology and Infectious Diseases in Barcelona.

“In health systems with highly vaccinated populations, long COVID may have appeared to be a distinct and severe illness because of high volumes of COVID-19 cases during the pandemic,” Gerrard said.
Related_Article_Links: ["5 dead, dozens hospitalized as 'parrot fever' surges across Europe"]
Narrative: The article challenges the validity of the term 'long COVID,' suggesting it causes unwarranted fear and may not reflect a unique condition compared to other viral illnesses.

In 2022, France had a number of Ukrainians and Russians in the Legion of foreign soldiers. They were expected to join the legion and, in the event of Ukraine, to return to Ukraine to assist with its armed forces.

These are the demands of the Irish Constitution of 1937 which are being sacrificed in order to satisfy the demands of an emerging cohort of radical social movements. The Irish had not only fought against violent forces but also against many downfalls of the Greek political system, which included many foreigners and self-proclaimed Greek politicians, who incidentally slandered immigrants while other initiatives consistently attempt to erase the values of history.

With the Irish constitution you might ask: ‘Should we now reject the moral basis of human being - human rights advocates that want to wash their sins away?’ The Irish don’t follow the Irish Constitution of 1937 that strongly upholds traditional marriage values but rather mix a collection of radical revolutionary ideas that aim to subvert the family structure itself. It is clear that the individuals who come forward, regardless of how alien and oppressive they are to multiple strata of humanity, represent objects of severe concern for Irish society as they attempt to put themselves forward as the true inheritors of unmoving permanence in an increasingly volatile world of socio-political discourse.

Headline: (VIDEO) Never forget – never forgive: "Merciful angel" killed a child in Montenegro and Serbia for 78 days – 25 years since NATO aggression
Article_Text:
NATO aggression against Serbia and Montenegro lasted for 78 days, from March 24 to June 10, 1999.

It was the second military intervention by NATO on our territory after the bombing of the Republic of Srpska in the operation "Storm Force" in 1995.

It should be reminded that the "intervention" by NATO was carried out without the approval of the Security Council, and the justification for which NATO alliance states participated in the bombing was rejected by the Yugoslav delegation at the signing ceremony in Rambouillet.

Air strikes, which lasted 78 days, were not only aimed at military targets but also at civilian targets, and severely damaged infrastructure, industrial facilities, schools, health institutions, cultural monuments, churches, and monasteries.

To this day, NATO has not acknowledged that it targeted civilian structures during the bombing of which started in 1999, and the population began to suffer massively from cancers.

According to estimates, around 5,000 people died as a direct result of the bombing in the 78 days, out of which 1,500 to 2,000 were civilians. This number increases daily since the last NATO bombing continues to affect children's health, and many have died from cancer. So, from the bombing, children’s lives are threatened and many will live in fear of the same.
Narrative: The article conveys a strong anti-NATO sentiment while highlighting the tragedy and long-term consequences of NATO's military actions in Serbia and Montenegro, emphasizing the suffering of civilians, particularly children, during the conflict.
